name: capitalism-and-freedom
description: Milton Friedman's Capitalism and Freedom — free-market economics and political philosophy toolkit arguing that economic freedom is necessary for political freedom, with proposals for school vouchers, negative income tax, monetarism, and limited government
useCase: Understand the connection between capitalism and political freedom, learn Friedman's policy proposals, and apply free-market principles to evaluate government intervention
knownRisks: Friedman's free-market prescriptions are not universally accepted and have been criticized by many economists
The book was written in 1962 — some specific policy contexts have changed
Friedman's positions (e.g., on corporate social responsibility, minimum wage) remain deeply controversial
